message:
test(fcp-ratelimit): proptest burst/capacity invariants for TokenBucket

Four new proptests (64 cases each = 256 trials) covering the
time-independent portion of the rate-limit contract. Verifies
TokenBucket via the sync private try_consume so no runtime or clock
mocking is needed:

  * prop_burst_cap_is_exactly_capacity — fresh bucket of capacity C
    permits EXACTLY C immediate try_consume(1) successes before any
    refill; (C+1)th fails. This is the burst half of the
    "count ≤ burst + window*rate" spec bound.
  * prop_permits_exceeding_capacity_always_fail — try_consume(n > cap)
    unconditionally fails and leaves state unchanged. Strict bound
    that prevents a single over-sized request from draining the bucket.
  * prop_zero_permits_is_state_neutral — try_consume(0) always
    succeeds and does not change remaining(), even on an empty bucket.
    Load-bearing for conditional-acquire call sites.
  * prop_partial_consume_leaves_exact_remaining — after consuming k < C
    from a fresh bucket, exactly (C - k) more try_consume(1) calls
    succeed before the next fails. Locks the arithmetic.

Time-dependent refill invariants (the "window*rate" half) remain
covered by the existing async tests in this module — lifting those
to proptest requires a mock clock and is out of scope.

Windows are set to 10 minutes so no refill interrupts the tight
consume loops, even under CI scheduling. Capacity caps at 10k to
keep total runtime bounded.

Added proptest to dev-dependencies (workspace-versioned).

Verified with
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-A cargo test \
    -p fcp-ratelimit --lib token_bucket
→ 69 passed, 0 failed (includes all 4 new proptests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,3 +39,4 @@ rand = "0.9"
 [dev-dependencies]
 ciborium.workspace = true
 uuid.workspace = true
+proptest.workspace = true
